title: Event sharing to groups with configurable visibility levels
body:
## Issue description

With the shift to personal event ownership, users need a way to share their events to one or more groups. Currently, events are created within a single group and all group members are automatically invited. The new model requires explicit, user-controlled sharing with configurable visibility levels.

Users should be able to:
- Share a personal event to one or more groups they belong to
- Choose what level of detail each group can see (busy, title, or full details)
- Update or revoke sharing at any time
- Share the same event to different groups with different visibility levels

---

## High-level requirements / Expected behavior

### 1. Domain layer

**New `EventShare` entity:**
```
EventShare {
  id: UUID
  eventId: UUID
  groupId: UUID
  visibilityLevel: 'busy' | 'title' | 'details'
  sharedByUserId: UUID
  sharedAt: Date
  updatedAt: Date
}
```

**Visibility levels:**
- **`busy`** — Group members see only that the event owner is busy during the time slot. No title, description, or other details are exposed. Display as "Busy" in calendar.
- **`title`** — Group members see the event title, start/end times, and status. No description, location, or collaboration details.
- **`details`** — Group members see full event information including title, description, start/end times, status, and location (if set). Access to event-scoped collaboration (chat, checklist) may be gated separately.

**Domain rules:**
- Only the event owner can share, update sharing, or revoke sharing
- User must be a member of the target group to share an event there
- An event can be shared to the same group only once (update visibility level, don't duplicate)
- Sharing does not grant edit access to the event — only view access at the specified visibility level
- Revoking a share removes the event from that group's view immediately

### 2. Application layer — use cases

- **ShareEventToGroupUseCase** — Share an event to a group with a specified visibility level
  - Validates: caller is event owner, caller is group member, event not already shared to this group
  - Creates `EventShare` record
  - Audits the action

- **UpdateEventShareUseCase** — Change the visibility level of an existing share
  - Validates: caller is event owner
  - Updates `visibility_level` and `updated_at`

- **RevokeEventShareUseCase** — Remove sharing from a group
  - Validates: caller is event owner
  - Deletes `EventShare` record
  - Audits the action

- **ListEventSharesUseCase** — List all groups an event is shared to (owner-only)
  - Returns list of `EventShare` with group names

### 3. Presentation layer — API endpoints

| Method | Endpoint | Purpose |
|--------|----------|----------|
| POST | `/api/v1/events/:eventId/shares` | Share event to a group |
| GET | `/api/v1/events/:eventId/shares` | List all shares for an event (owner-only) |
| PATCH | `/api/v1/events/:eventId/shares/:shareId` | Update visibility level |
| DELETE | `/api/v1/events/:eventId/shares/:shareId` | Revoke sharing |

**Request body for POST:**
```json
{
  "groupId": "uuid",
  "visibilityLevel": "busy" | "title" | "details"
}
```

**Request body for PATCH:**
```json
{
  "visibilityLevel": "busy" | "title" | "details"
}
```

### 4. Client-side sharing UI

- Event detail page gets a "Share" action/button for the event owner
- Share dialog shows groups the user belongs to with a visibility level selector for each
- Already-shared groups show their current visibility level with options to change or revoke
- Sharing state is visually indicated on the event (e.g., share icon with count)

### 5. Privacy and data minimization

- When a group member views a shared event, the API must respect the visibility level:
  - `busy` → return only `{ id, ownerId, startAt, endAt, visibilityLevel: 'busy' }` — no title, no description
  - `title` → return `{ id, ownerId, title, startAt, endAt, status, visibilityLevel: 'title' }` — no description, no collaboration data
  - `details` → return full event data as allowed by the detail level
- The API must never return more data than the visibility level permits, regardless of what the client requests
- Unshared events must not appear in any group view or leak existence

---

## Additional notes

- Depends on: Database schema migration, Personal event management
- The `event_shares` table is defined in the schema migration issue
- Sharing is orthogonal to event invitations — sharing controls visibility to a group, invitations are for RSVP tracking within the event
- Consider how sharing interacts with event collaboration features (chat, checklist, location) — this may warrant a separate issue if complex
- Audit logging required for share, update, and revoke actions
- Follow existing module patterns (domain → application → infrastructure → presentation)

### Related files
- `src/modules/event-management/` — extend or create sub-module
- `src/modules/audit-security/` — audit integration
- `client/src/api/events.ts` — client API
- `client/src/pages/EventDetail/` — event detail page

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `EventShare` domain entity with `eventId`, `groupId`, `visibilityLevel`, `sharedByUserId`
- [ ] Visibility levels: `busy`, `title`, `details` with correct data filtering for each
- [ ] Event owner can share to any group they belong to
- [ ] Event owner can update visibility level of an existing share
- [ ] Event owner can revoke sharing from a group
- [ ] Non-owners cannot share, update, or revoke shares
- [ ] Shared events appear in group views respecting visibility levels
- [ ] `busy` level returns no title, description, or collaboration data
- [ ] `title` level returns title and times but no description or collaboration data
- [ ] `details` level returns full event information
- [ ] Unshared events do not appear in group views and do not leak existence
- [ ] Same event can be shared to multiple groups with different visibility levels
- [ ] API endpoints validate input and return appropriate error responses
- [ ] Client UI allows sharing, updating visibility, and revoking from event detail page
- [ ] Audit logs recorded for share, update, and revoke actions
- [ ] Unit tests cover sharing domain rules and visibility filtering
- [ ] Integration tests cover event share persistence
- [ ] API tests cover all sharing endpoints (happy path, authorization, validation)
- [ ] Privacy tests verify data minimization per visibility level
- [ ] CI pipeline passes
